spec.reasoning: The task references specific cell addresses (I12, J23) which means any row or column shifting operations (blank_row_insert, title_row_insert, column_reorder) would break the cell references in the instruction. The risk flag 'task_references_specific_cell_addresses' confirms this — the previous attempt failed exactly because blank_row_insert shifted the referenced rows. Instead, I use safe perturbations: (1) Rename columns 'Client' → 'Customer' and 'Deal #' → 'Transaction #' — these are semantically equivalent and neither term appears as general English in the instruction. (2) Rename the sheet from 'Deal 8' to 'Transaction 8' — the instruction never references the sheet by name, so this only tests whether the model hardcodes sheet names. (3) Add a distractor column 'Deal Ref' after the last column — this is plausibly similar to the renamed 'Transaction #' but clearly different, and being appended at the end it does not shift any existing columns. No blank_row_insert is used to avoid the row-shifting problem identified in the failed attempt.
